title: System Model: Input–Output Description
summary: This page demonstrates the use of differential operator D notation to express input-output equations for linear systems. It includes Example 1.17, which derives the input-output equation for a series RC circuit (R=15Ω, C=1/5 F) using operator notation, showing the loop equation expressed both in integral form and operator form.

figures.caption: An RC series circuit with a voltage source x(t), resistor R = 15 Ω, and capacitor C = 1/5 F, used to find the loop current i(t) and capacitor voltage y(t).
figures.description: The figure shows a single-loop RC series circuit with an input voltage source labeled x(t), a resistor labeled R = 15 Ω on the top branch, and a capacitor labeled C = 1/5 F on the right branch. A curved arrow inside the loop marks the loop current i(t), and the output voltage y(t) is measured across the capacitor with polarity labels + at the top terminal and − at the bottom terminal. There are no plotted axes; the key relationship is that the same current flows through the resistor and capacitor in series, while y(t) is the capacitor voltage.
